gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/220)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/220)
text: ذَكَرَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْبَصَلَ فَقَالَ يُطَيِّبُ النَّكْهَةَ وَ يَذْهَبُ بِالْبَلْغَمِ وَ يَزِيدُ فِي الْجِمَاعِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from sahl Bin Ziyad, from Mansour Bin Al Abbas, from Abdul Aziz bin Hassan Al Baghdady, from Salih Bin Uqba, from Abdullah Bin Muhammad Al Ju’fy who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> mentioned the onions, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It aromatises the breath, and does away with the phlegm, and increases in the copulation’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Mansur ibn al-‘Abbas from ‘Abd ‘Abd al-’Aziz ibn Hassan from Salih ibn ‘Uqbah from ‘Abd Allah ibn Muhammad al-Al-Ju‘fiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once mentioned onion and said, ‘It is well flavored and it dissolves phlegm and increases one’s ability to go to bed with his wife.’”
